Empires & Puzzles Zarel Review: The Bard of Rush Tournaments

Is Zarel just a backup singer or the lead star of your 3-star team? We break down how this Green Bard's unique 'Block Cleanse' skill and passive mana generation make her a silent powerhouse in Rush Tournaments, effectively locking down enemies and supercharging her allies.
Zarel Review: The Tambourine Queen of Rush Attack
Zarel: The Tambourine Queen of Rush Attack
Zarel enters the stage as part of the
from the Challenge Festival II event. Released back in late 2022, she brought a mechanic previously reserved for elite 5-star heroes down to the humble 3-star arena: the dreaded
While she is technically a "Slow" hero, don't let that fool you. Zarel wasn't designed for standard play; she was designed to break the rules of

tournaments. As a Paladin with a support kit wrapped in an AoE damage package, she serves a very specific, very deadly niche. If you are tired of your debuffs being wiped away by a pesky Faiez or Helo, Zarel is the bandmate you've been looking for.

Zarel’s design is pure chaotic bard energy. She’s a goblin-esque musician mid-performance, wielding a tambourine that glows with magical green energy. The art captures her mischievous nature perfectly—she looks like she’s about to drop a beat that will literally hurt you. The vibrant greens and playful expression fit well with the "Festival" theme, standing out against the usually grim and gritty warriors of the game. It's nice to see someone actually enjoying the battle for once!
When we look at Zarel's stats through the lens of
, she is definitely showing her age.
Modest. Modern 3-star damage dealers (like the Construct or Shadow heroes) are pushing well into the 530-550 range.
This is where the "power creep" hits hardest. Newer 3-stars like
boast health pools over 900 or even 1000. Zarel is comparatively squishy and relies heavily on her Paladin class and emblems to survive long enough to fire.
However, unlike pure snipers, Zarel's value lies in her
, not just her raw numbers. She doesn't need to have the highest attack stat to lock down an enemy team.
is a deceptively powerful skill that does three things at once:
260% to all enemies is a respectable slap, especially for a 3-star. It softens up the entire enemy team for your heavy hitters.
-34% attack for 5 turns cripples the enemy's ability to retaliate. In 3-star tournaments where slash attacks can be lethal, this adds significant survivability to your team.
This is the crown jewel. "All enemies can't get their status ailments cleansed."
(Defense Down, Attack Down, Mana Slow) are king. The usual counter is to bring a cleanser like
. Zarel shuts that door. If Zarel fires, your opponent is stuck with those debilitating ailments for the full duration. It turns a "bad situation" into a "game over" for the enemy.
It is important to distinguish between "Cleansing" and "Flipping" or "Overwriting". While Zarel stops a cleanser from

the ailment, certain mechanics (like a buff that specifically flips ailments to buffs) might still interact with this, depending on the specific wording and game version updates. However, for the vast majority of 3-star cleansers, Zarel is a hard stop.
(see below). She grants +5% mana generation to "Solo" heroes.
In a Rush tournament, this 5% bonus is often the tipping point that allows your
heroes (especially Average speed ones) to charge in
instead of 7, provided they have the right mana troop support. This mana break-point manipulation is a high-level strategy that wins tournaments.
At roughly 690 Attack (Limit Broken), a 260% hit deals significant damage across the board. While she won't one-shot a team like a fully charged
, she contributes a heavy chunk of damage (approx. 300-400 damage per hero depending on defense) while applying her nasty effects.

Bard on your team, she counts as a "Solo" hero and grants herself (and any other hero who is alone in their family)

This passive reduces the effect of defense-down ailments by
Enemy Treevil hits you with -44% defense.
That becomes only -22% defense for Zarel and her allies.
Your team survives the follow-up hits much better. This is a massive hidden survival tool against the Defense Down meta.

is a solid, if unexciting, power for her. Since she is likely to be taking heavy fire in a tank or flank position, the passive healing helps keep her alive long enough to cast her skill or survive until a healer fires.
When Zarel was released, she was immediately identified as a "Rush Tournament Specialist." The community debated whether she made the already oppressive
meta even worse by making his ailments uncleansable. (Spoiler: She did).
Zarel has faced significant power creep in the stat department. The arrival of
means she is facing enemies with vastly superior stats. However, her
have not been crept. There is still no other 3-star hero who offers an AoE Block Cleanse combined with a universal mana generation buff.
Slow speed without the stats to back it up makes her a liability in standard raid formats or story modes.
She remains a staple in top-tier defenses and attacks. Her ability to speed up allies and lock in ailments makes her timeless in this specific mode.
As of this review, Zarel does not have a costume. This review focuses on her base form.
Zarel needs to fire once to be effective. Since she is Slow (outside of Rush) or a high-priority target (in Rush), survival is paramount.
Maximizes her survivability. The Paladin class talent (Defense Up on damage) synergizes perfectly with high defense stats.
She is already squishy by modern standards; this build risks her dying before casting.
. Her primary job is to debuff the enemy and buff your team's mana. The damage is just a bonus.
Prevents enemies from recovering from devastating ailments.
Passive +5% mana gen for "solo" allies helps hit tile breakpoints.
Passive 50% reduction to defense-down ailments keeps the team alive.
Absolute monster in Very Fast modes.
agonizingly slow in normal speed game modes.
Low health/defense compared to 2025/2026 heroes creates a weak point in defenses.
Really only shines in Rush Attack or very specific PvE stages.
Her natural habitat. She is an S-tier pick here.
If you are using 3-stars in war (cleanup teams), she is excellent.
The -34% Attack debuff and AoE damage are very strong here, though her Slow speed is a hindrance.
This is a classic "You lose" defense.
boosts everyone's mana (if they are solo/diverse) and blocks the enemy from cleansing.
If Zarel fires after Treevil, the enemy is stuck with -Def, -Atk, and Slow Mana for the rest of the match.
Stopping Zarel from firing is the best way to deal with her.
If you can block her from gaining buffs, her Paladin talent is useless, though this doesn't stop her skill.
Putting another Bard on the team disables her "Solo" mana bonus for both of them (unless you have 2+ unique families, but usually you want the Solo bonus for everyone).

If you pull Zarel, lock her immediately. She is not a hero you will use for map farming or your main raid team, but she will single-handedly win you gems and emblems in 3-Star Rush Tournaments. Level her up, but don't feel pressured to Limit Break her unless you are serious about tournament ranking.
You likely already know her value. The question is:
Is she worth the Limit Break 2 (Alpha Aether)?

If you compete for Top 1% in tournaments, the stats from LB2 help her survive the modern high-damage snipers. If you are casual about tournaments, she works fine at LB1 or even just max level. Her utility doesn't change with stats.
When Zarel dropped, the community reaction was immediate and slightly terrified.
"Is it me, or did Treevil just get more powerful in 3
Rush tourneys?"* — One user correctly predicted the nightmare combo.
There was also significant confusion regarding her skill translation in other languages, with some German players thinking she
status changes instead of blocking the cleanse!
A mini-version of a powerhouse 5-star, which is high praise for a Rare hero.

Zarel is the perfect example of a hero who punches above her weight class thanks to a unique skill set. She might be holding a tambourine, but in a Rush Tournament, she's the one orchestrating the enemy's demise. She effectively staples debilitating ailments to your opponents and ensures they stay there. While her stats are gathering dust, her toolkit remains shiny and dangerous.

A mandatory keeper for tournament enthusiasts, but a bench-warmer for everything else. Keep her solo, keep her loud, and watch the enemy team struggle to clear their throats—and their status ailments.
A detailed review and strategy guide for Zarel, the 3-star Nature Bard hero. Analysis of her stats, special skill, and why she is a top-tier pick for Rush Attack tournaments.
